business.headline: Maharashtra Day on May 1: Is the stock market open or closed today?The BSE and NSE are closed on May 1 for Maharashtra Day across key trading segments. Commodity derivatives will reopen in the evening, while traders should account for settlement and liquidity effects.

Commercial, mini LPG prices hiked Rs 993 and Rs 261, no change in domestic ratesCommercial LPG cylinder prices have surged by Rs 993 to Rs 3,071.50 in Delhi, while domestic LPG, petrol, diesel, and kerosene rates remain unchanged to shield consumers.

Bajaj Finance raises FD rates up to 7.40%: Should you lock in now?The company has increased rates by up to 45 basis points, with the biggest jump seen in longer tenures. This means investors looking to park money for a few years could now earn better returns.

Markets end lower as crude stays high, rupee hits record lowMarkets closed lower on Thursday as high crude prices and a weak rupee weighed on sentiment. The slide highlighted persistent inflation and capital flow concerns, even as IT and pharma offered limited support.

Bajaj Finserv announces dividend, reports strong FY26 growthBajaj Finserv reported record FY26 income and profit and announced a special dividend with its Q4 results. The update also marked the completion of Allianz's stake exit, leaving its insurance businesses fully Bajaj-owned.

India-NZ FTA opens doors, but only prepared firms will benefitSiddharth Shankar says the India-New Zealand FTA offers big openings for trade and talent. He argues only firms with strong systems, compliance and execution will truly benefit.

8th Pay Commission: What worker bodies have demanded ahead of salary reviewWorker representatives laid out pay, pension and service demands in the first 8th Pay Commission meeting in Delhi. The staff side said the talks ended positively but pressed for wider consultation and more meetings.

OnEMI Technology IPO: Weak Day 1 response, is it risky for long-term investors?Kissht IPO was subscribed just 0.25 times on Day 1, indicating a slow start. The retail portion saw particularly weak interest, with subscriptions at only 0.06 times.

Where should a 25-year-old invest to build wealth: SIPs, stocks or crypto?At 25, with time on your side, what's the smartest way to invest, i.e., SIPs (Systematic Investment Plans), stocks or crypto? Let's have a look.
Big EPFO update: Minimum pension may rise 7.5 times, ATM withdrawals soonThe government is mulling a sharp increase in the minimum pension under the Employees' Pension Scheme (EPS-95), say sources.

From TDS mismatches to capital gains errors: 3 risks under Income Tax Act 2025The Income Tax Act, 2025 is now in force, and for many companies, the transition seems smooth so far. But could small gaps like TDS mismatches and capital gains errors turn into bigger compliance issues?

Rupee sinks to record low as oil spike and Fed stance pile on pressureThe rupee slipped to a record low against the US dollar as crude oil prices surged and the Federal Reserve stayed hawkish. The move underlines pressure from India's rising import bill, firmer dollar demand and tighter global financial conditions.

Sensex down 400 points: Why is the stock market falling today?The BSE Sensex was down 1,215.91 points, or 1.54%, at 76,280.45 around 10:58 am, while the Nifty 50 fell 366.70 points, or 1.51%, to 23,811.25.
Sensex opens 700 points lower, Nifty below 24,000; IndiGo down 2%The &P BSE Sensex was down 947.08 points to 76,549.28, while the NSE Nifty50 lost 317.60 points to 23,860.05 as of 10:01 am.

JPMorgan asked Vis Raghavan to go, Citi snapped him up for $52 million in 2 daysVis Raghavan moved from JPMorgan Chase to Citigroup's top investment banking role over a single weekend in February 2024. The rapid switch, later detailed by the Financial Times, sharpened attention on his exit and his rising clout at Citi.

Infosys won't cut jobs despite AI shift, says CEO Salil ParekhInfosys CEO Salil Parekh said the company does not plan layoffs despite the AI shift. The stance sets it apart from peers and underscores a hiring-and-reskilling push.

Crude oil tops $120, highest since 2022: What it means for markets and rupeeDisruptions around the Strait of Hormuz, a key oil transit route, have added to the uncertainty, taking crude oil prices to their highest levels since mid-2022.

8th Pay Commission update: Has the last date been extended amid employee demands?The Commission is holding a three-day consultation session from April 28 to April 30. During these meetings, representatives from various employee groups are discussing key issues related to pay, allowances and service conditions.
Oil hits $115: Prices surge to highest since 2022 amid Iran tensionsOngoing tensions around Iran and fears of supply disruptions are pushing crude prices to levels not seen in nearly two years.

Rajiv Bajaj to step down from Bajaj Finance board at end of termRajiv Bajaj's tenure as a non-executive director will conclude at the close of the company's upcoming annual general meeting, in line with regulatory requirements.

entertainment.headline: Where did the plot go wrong for Rajeev Khandelwal? Rajeev Khandelwal has returned to TV as the host of Tum Ho Naa - Ghar Ki Superstar, a reality show that doesn't seem to do justice to his stature as a small screen superstar. The move brings back questions about why a widely praised actor never managed to convert acclaim into lasting commercial momentum.

Pregnant Deepika shoots King with SRK, fans call leaked pics pure Renaissance magic Leaked pictures from King's South African schedule showed Deepika Padukone and Shah Rukh Khan walking hand in hand on set. The visuals sparked strong fan reactions as Deepika continued filming after announcing her second pregnancy.

Evangeline Lilly slams Disney, says AI is replacing artists who built the MCU Ant Man's Evangeline Lilly criticised Disney's latest layoffs after Marvel artist Andy Park said he had been let go. She said the cuts sidelined the artists who shaped the MCU and raised fears about AI replacing creative work.

Michael, Elvis, Bohemian Rhapsody: Three musical lives, one cinematic impact With Michael now in theatres, comparisons with Elvis and Bohemian Rhapsody are inevitable—different in style yet united in resonance, as all three continue to strike a chord with music lovers across audiences

Met Gala 2026 Indian guests: KJo to Deepika and Priyanka. When and where to watch Ahead of Met Gala 2026 on May 4, curiosity is building around the line-up of Indian celebrities. Apart from Karan Johar, Deepika Padukone, Ranveer Singh, Priyanka Chopra Jonas, Alia Bhatt and several others are expected to attend the global fashion event.

BTS ARIRANG comeback fuels HYBE's record-breaking quarter. Details HYBE posted its highest-ever first-quarter revenue after BTS returned with ARIRANG. The surge spread across touring, merchandise and Weverse, showing the strength of its fan ecosystem.

Bombay High Court dismisses PIL against Raja Shivaji title, no stay on film release The Bombay High Court refused to stay the release of Raja Shivaji over objections to its title. The bench said the late PIL was motivated and found nothing derogatory in the film's name.

Karan Johar to debut at Met Gala 2026? Here's what we know Karan Johar is reportedly set to attend the Met Gala 2026, with official confirmation still awaited. His expected debut comes as Indian names continue to gain visibility at the global fashion event.

That's allRelease Date:Fri May 01, 2026 The Devil Wears Prada 2 review: Nearly two decades later, Miranda Priestly returns as Runway fights for relevance in a digital-first media world. The sequel uses that struggle to examine how fashion, journalism and influence now bend before metrics.

Priyanka, Katrina or Nayanthara: Are Tumbbad 2 makers eyeing an A-list name? Tumbbad 2 is exploring a leading female star for a pivotal role as work on the sequel moves ahead. The talks add fresh intrigue to the film, which is set for December 2027 theatrical release.

Office Romance trailer: Jennifer Lopez leads a risky love story with Brett Goldstein The trailer for Office Romance introduces Jennifer Lopez and Brett Goldstein as colleagues drawn to each other at work. It sets up a choice between following their feelings and protecting the company Jackie has built.

Pavitra Punia shuts down trolls over Mouni Roy surgery claims Pavitra Punia has defended Mouni Roy and lashed out at trolls over surgery speculation. In a video on social media, she said personal choices should be respected and accused suggestive captions of fuelling needless chatter.

Kareena reacts after Karisma's children get relief in Sunjay Kapur's asset case Kareena Kapoor extended support to her sister Karisma by sharing a hopeful message after the Delhi High Court ordered status quo on Sunjay Kapur's estate. The ruling preserves the assets during the will dispute and places Priya Kapur's claims under closer scrutiny.

Pagglait 2 confirmed: Sanya Malhotra returns, shoot to begin in August Pagglait 2 has been greenlit, with Sanya Malhotra and director Umesh Bist returning. The sequel adds another follow-up title to Netflix India's slate.

Kartavya: SRK shares release date of his cop drama starring Saif Ali Khan. See date Saif Ali Khan's cop drama Kartavya is finally set to premiere on Netflix. Shah Rukh Khan shared the release date of the drama backed by his production house, Red Chillies Entertainment.

KD The Devil X review: Mass love for Dhruva Sarja, fans call film absolute rampage KD: The Devil has opened to strong fan chatter on X and mixed critical reviews. The split response turns attention to its stronger second half, theatrical highs and uneven screenplay.

At 76, Nana Patekar's tricep dips leave the internet impressed A video of actor Nana Patekar doing tricep dips has gone viral on social media. Celebrities, including Shilpa Shetty and Badshah, have reacted to the clip on Instagram.

Samantha's Maa Inti Bangaram to skip May 15 release? Here's what we know Samantha's Maa Inti Bangaram may be postponed from its May 15 release amid IPL scheduling concerns. The reported June 4 plan has stirred fan curiosity, though the makers have not confirmed any change.

Sonali Kulkarni shares 5-hour Mumbai traffic nightmare: This is scary Sonali Kulkarni said she was stranded for more than five hours in a traffic jam on Mumbai's Mankhurd bridge while travelling to Pune. Sharing a video from the spot, she called the situation scary and asked the police for an update.

general news.headline: Woman found clutching 4-year-old son after Jabalpur boat tragedyRescuers at the site of the Jabalpur boat tragedy recovered the bodies of a mother and her young son, still locked in a final embrace.

Mamata Banerjee warns of 'life-and-death' fight if EVMs are tampered withMamata Banerjee visited the EVM strongroom at Sakhawat Memorial School after reports of alleged irregularities. Her visit followed Trinamool Congress protests over ballot handling and warnings against any tampering.

Little brown boy: JPMorgan exec accused of drugging, sexually abusing stafferA former JPMorgan employee, who is an Asian, has sued executive director Lorna Hajdini, alleging sexual abuse, racial harassment and intimidation. The bank has denied the claims, saying no evidence against Hajdini was found after an internal probe.

Where exactly is leftover of the Left in India?From ruling three states at a time to surviving in scattered pockets, India's Communist parties are facing an existential crisis. Exit polls suggest the Left front could lose Kerala, the only state it governs now. The question is not if the Left exists, but where it exists in India.

White House event shooter seen storming security, opening fire in new CCTV footageSecurity camera footage released by Jeanine Pirro shows Cole Tomas Allen running through a checkpoint and shooting a federal agent at the Washington Hilton during the White House Correspondents' Association dinner.

We raised alarm, crew paid no heed: Jabalpur boat tragedy survivor recounts horrorA boat carrying 30 people capsized at Bargi Dam in Jabalpur amid sudden strong winds, killing nine and leaving several missing, despite an earlier weather alert warning of hazardous conditions.

Live: Losers tend to complain, BJP hits back at TMC over EVM tampering chargesWest Bengal Exit Polls Live Updates: A controversy has erupted over strongrooms after TMC alleged ballot tampering, citing a viral video, while the Election Commission denied wrongdoing, saying all EVMs and ballots are securely sealed and monitored.

Sikotar Mata: How ancient Indian mariners carried home a Yemeni island as a deityAncient Indian mariners worshipped a Yemeni island and brought it back home as a deity. The island of Socotra, near East Africa, is believed to have manifested as Sikotar Mata, a sea goddess worshipped in Gujarat even today. Socotra was a vital refuge in the Arabian Sea for maritime traders, over 2,000 km from home.

Godrej Industries lays out 2031 growth ambitionsThe purpose statement, 'Crafting tomorrow since 1897', celebrates the Godrej Group's legacy while clearly positioning the new, refreshed Godrej Industries for the future.

Inside EC's Delhi war room: How CEC chief tracked Bengal vote hourlyThe Election Commission, led by Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar, ran a real-time war room to monitor West Bengal polling through hourly reviews and live webcasts. Alerts triggered swift intervention on the ground, ensuring rapid response, preventing irregularities, and reinforcing the promise of free, fair, and intimidation-free elections across sensitive booths.

Parag who was fired by Elon Musk, soon after he bought Twitter in 2022, has raised $100 million for his new AI startup called Parallel Web Systems, taking its valuation to around Rs 19,020 crore.

Hyderabad techie dies by suicide, blames wife's 'multiple affairs' in noteA 36-year-old software engineer reportedly drowned after jumping into a lake after accusing his wife of multiple affairs. He left a 19-page note, claiming his wife had affairs with three men.

BRICS at risk of imploding, reveals Delhi meetThere is a wide rift in the BRICS, of which India is a founding member, due to the war in the Middle East. There was no joint statement after the recent meeting in New Delhi that was described as "very tense". India as the BRICS president will have to do a tight rope walk ahead of the crucial summit in September.

South Africa scraps its debut AI policy after it was found written by AISouth Africa's debut AI policy collapsed within weeks after fabricated, AI-generated citations exposed a critical oversight failure.

Vijay's TVK the disruptor-in-chief? Today's Chanakya exit poll predicts 63 seatsThe Today's Chanakya exit poll projects a strong debut for TVK, estimating 63 ± 11 seats and a 30 per cent (±3 per cent) vote share.

Rain, thunder and dust storms: Which states get hit hardest today?May Day will bring heavy rain and lightning to east India, a hot dry spell to Delhi and continued pre-monsoon showers across the south. Here is a state-by-state breakdown of what to expect.

sports.headline: How has Holder filled the Hardik Pandya void for GT? Vikram Solanki explains Jason Holder's all-round display against Royal Challengers Bengaluru powered Gujarat Titans' win and earned warm praise from Vikram Solanki. As Gujarat push for the playoffs, Holder provides balance, experience and communication to fill the gap left by Hardik Pandya's exit in 2024.

Lakshya Sen, Ayush Shetty and Kidambi Srikanth will play the singles matches for India on Friday. Here's all you need to know about the big quarterfinal tie.

Riyan Parag vaping row a reminder players need to maintain culture: Sangakkara Kumar Sangakkara said Rajasthan Royals' recent controversies have been addressed internally, calling them a reminder for players to uphold team culture ahead of their clash against Delhi Capitals.

Gujarat scratching their heads over middle order woes? Rashid Khan opens up IPL 2026, GT vs RCB: Rashid Khan said Gujarat Titans should not overthink their inconsistent middle order, stressing that T20 cricket exposes every team's weaknesses. On Thursday, Rashid took two wickets as GT beat RCB by four wickets in Ahmedabad.

How fearless Shubman Gill sucked the venom out of RCB pace attack IPL 2026, GT vs RCB: Jos Buttler revealed that Shubman Gill planned a bold counterattack against Bhuvneshwar Kumar and Josh Hazlewood, as Gujarat Titans beat Royal Challengers Bengaluru by 4 wickets on Thursday in Ahmedabad.

Gujarat Titans take sweet revenge as Bengaluru endure rare off day IPL 2026, GT vs RCB: Gujarat Titans beat the Royal Challengers Bengaluru by four wickets at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad. Chasing 156, Shubman Gill's men romped home in 15.5 overs.

Virat Kohli gives Shubman Gill death stare, fiery send-off in Ahmedabad: Watch GT vs RCB, IPL 2026: Virat Kohli brought the intensity with an animated send-off to Shubman Gill, but Gujarat Titans stayed on top after Kagiso Rabada and Jason Holder dented RCB in Ahmedabad.

Virat Kohli hits out at umpires after Jason Holder's catch to dismiss Rajat Patidar GT vs RCB, IPL 2026: Virat Kohli was not happy after Rajat Patidar's controversial dismissal against Gujarat Titans, with replays raising questions over Jason Holder's catch and the third umpire's review.

Virat Kohli dominates Kagiso Rabada with boundary blitz, GT pacer answers with revenge IPL 2026, GT vs RCB: Virat Kohli dominated Kagiso Rabada early with a stunning burst of five consecutive boundaries in IPL 2026, but the GT pacer fought back strongly. Rabada had the last laugh by dismissing Kohli for 28.

Was Riyan Parag let off after vaping? Fans call out hypocrisy after escaping ban Riyan Parag's dressing-room vaping incident has sparked backlash after the IPL fined him 20 per cent of his match fee, with fans questioning whether the Rajasthan Royals captain was punished lightly.

MCC defends umpires in Angkrish Raghuvanshi Obstructing the Field dismissal callThe MCC defended the umpire's decision to give Angkrish Raghuvanshi out obstructing the field against Lucknow Super Giants, stating it aligns with long-standing laws that penalise wilful changes of running direction.

Indonesia's Thomas Cup exit, a historic first in 68 yearsIndonesia's shock Thomas Cup group-stage exit ends a 68-year run of dominance, exposing a rare crack in badminton's most enduring empire and possibly marking a defining shift in the sport's power balance.

Lungi Ngidi returns to bowling for Delhi capitals after recovering from head injuryIPL 2026: Lungi Ngidi has returned to Delhi Capitals training after the head injury he suffered against Punjab Kings. The footage offers relief, but concussion protocol could still keep him out of the Rajasthan Royals match.

GT vs RCB Highlights: Gujarat beat Bengaluru by 4 wickets in AhmedabadGujarat (GT) vs Bengaluru (RCB) Highlights, IPL 2026: Gujarat Titans earned two crucial points after beating the Royal Challengers Bengaluru by four wickets on Thursday at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad.

Jensi Kanabar, 14, rewrites history books, surpasses Sania Mirza's all-time recordJensi Kanabar became the youngest Indian female to reach a professional singles quarter-final at 14 years and five months, surpassing Sania Mirza's long-standing record with a composed run at the ITF W15 New Delhi.

Cristiano Ronaldo five UCL gesture goes viral after Al Ahli fans' title jibeCristiano Ronaldo answered Al Ahli fans by holding up five fingers after Al Nassr's 2-0 win. The exchange went viral and sharpened the bad blood around a match already clouded by refereeing complaints and on-pitch tension.

Release Hardik Pandya: Mumbai Indians told to reset after 3 mellow years in IPLSimon Doull has urged Mumbai Indians to consider releasing Hardik Pandya if they don't think he will be able to lead the team into the next cycle. Mumbai Indians have had a tough time in IPL 2026. They are rooted to the 9th spot with just two wins in eight games.

Fresh trouble for Riyan Parag? RR captain escapes ban, but women's group seeks probeA women's group has sought an inquiry into visuals purportedly showing Riyan Parag with a vape-like device during an IPL match. It says the incident could normalise a banned product among young viewers and wants accountability.

Riyan Parag escapes ban for vaping during IPL match, fined by BCCIIPL 2026: The Board of Control for Cricket in India has fined Riyan Parag 25% of his match fee for vaping during an IPL match in Mullanpur, with the Rajasthan Royals captain escaping a stricter punishment.

Watch: Venkatesh Prasad's child-like joy in Bengaluru hailstorm, tastes it tooFormer India cricketer Venkatesh Prasad stopped his car during Bengaluru's sudden hailstorm and shared a playful video online. The clip struck a chord as the rare spell cooled the city and briefly turned parts of it white.

IPL business first, cricket next? Sri Lanka great's massive claim on run-festsIPL 2026: Sunrisers Hyderabad's spin bowling coach Muttiah Muralitharan said flat wickets and the IPL's design are fuelling relentless 200-plus totals. He argued the league prioritises entertainment, sponsors and viewer interest over cricket development after SRH's victory over MI with eight balls to spare.

Travishek Blueprint: The simple secret behind IPL's most destructive opening pairIPL 2026: Travis Head and Abhishek Sharma powered Sunrisers Hyderabad's chase of 245 against Mumbai Indians with another explosive opening stand. Their contrasting strengths and uncomplicated method continue to drive SRH's winning run.

Call Klaasen and add him to 2027 World Cup squad, Pietersen tells South AfricaIPL 2026: Kevin Pietersen has urged Cricket South Africa to convince Heinrich Klaasen to reverse his retirement, citing his match-winning form for Sunrisers Hyderabad. Klaasen, has been in a sensational form in the IPL scoring 414 runs at a strike-rate of 15741, placing him second in the Orange Cap race behind teammate Abhishek Sharma.

Gujarat Titans hope to erase mistakes of the past vs red-hot RCBGT vs RCB, IPL 2026 preview: Gujarat Titans are set to face Royal Challengers Bengaluru in their reverse fixture on Thursday. GT need to come out of their shell and adapt the modern T20 template if they want to beat a red-hot RCB.

Champions League: Arteta cries foul over VAR call as Arsenal held by AtleticoChampions League semi-final first leg: Coach Mikel Arteta hit out after Arsenal's penalty was overturned by VAR in Madrid. He said the decision denied a potentially decisive win.

Bumrah entitled to have a bad IPL season: Kieron Pollard's big message to criticsIPL 2026: Kieron Pollard defended Jasprit Bumrah after the Mumbai Indians pacer returned 0 for 54 against Sunrisers Hyderabad at Wankhede Stadium on Wednesday. His remarks highlighted the scrutiny on cricketers as Bumrah's lean IPL 2026 run continued.

Video: Vinod Kambli's Wankhede appearance during IPL match warms heartsIPL 2026: Vinod Kambli made a rare appearance at Wankhede Stadium during Mumbai Indians' match against Sunrisers Hyderabad on Wednesday, acknowledging fans who wished him well. The outing eased fresh health concerns and highlighted the support around the former India batter.

IPL Play of the Day: SRH prove T20 is a bowlers' game too with Wankhede masterclassMI vs SRH, IPL 2026: Sunrisers turned the game at the death, restricting Mumbai Indians to 243 when the home side looked set to cross 260. Ehsan Malinga shone in the final overs, while Travis Head and Abhishek Sharma later laid the foundation for a record chase at Wankhede Stadium on Wednesday.

After teammates, Hardik Pandya snaps at MI fans for supporting opponentsIPL 2026: Hardik Pandya has now turned his focus from teammates to the stands, calling out Mumbai Indians fans for backing opposition teams during this season. The MI captain admitted it "hurts" hearing rival chants at Wankhede after another disappointing home defeat.

No respite for MI as 243 not enough against rampant SRH at WankhedeIPL 2026: SunRisers Hyderabad extended their winning run in the Indian Premier League by recording the 4th highest chase in the history of the tournament. Chasing 244, Heinrich Klaasen anchored the SRH innings and took the team over the line in just 18.4 overs on Wednesday.

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
